name: abab6.5 / abab6.5s
tagline: MiniMax's 2024 flagship: a trillion-parameter MoE chat model with a 200K-token context window.
seoTitle: MiniMax abab6.5 & abab6.5s — 200K MoE Chat Model
metaDescription: MiniMax abab6.5 and abab6.5s, launched April 17, 2024: a trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts chat model with a 200K-token context window and a faster abab6.5…
note: Trillion-param MoE; 200K context; faster abab6.5s variant
overview: abab6.5 and abab6.5s are MiniMax's April 2024 flagship large language models, launched on April 17, 2024. abab6.5 is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) chat model that MiniMax describes as having roughly a trillion total parameters and supporting a 200,000-token context window. It was positioned as a general-purpose model for Chinese and multilingual chat, reasoning, and long-document tasks.

abab6.5s is the efficiency-focused sibling. It is trained with the same techniques and data as abab6.5 but is optimized for speed: MiniMax says it can process close to 30,000 words of text in about one second, while keeping the same 200,000-token context window. The pair gave developers a choice between the larger abab6.5 for maximum quality and abab6.5s for low-latency, high-throughput workloads.

The abab6.5 series was MiniMax's last major abab-branded generation before the company pivoted to its MiniMax-01 long-context line in January 2025 and later the open-weight M-series. The abab models were served through MiniMax's API platform rather than released as open weights, and have since been superseded; treat this page as a historical record of the legacy abab line.
strengths: 200,000-token context window, large for its 2024 release window
Mixture-of-Experts architecture with roughly a trillion total parameters
abab6.5s variant tuned for high speed and throughput
Strong Chinese-language and multilingual chat capability
useCases: Long-document analysis and summarization within a 200K context
High-throughput chat workloads via the faster abab6.5s
General reasoning and writing tasks through the MiniMax API

links.label: The Decoder — MiniMax record context length models

faq.q: When were abab6.5 and abab6.5s released?
faq.a: MiniMax officially launched the abab6.5 series on April 17, 2024. The series included two models: abab6.5 and the faster abab6.5s, both built on a Mixture-of-Experts architecture.

faq.q: What is the difference between abab6.5 and abab6.5s?
faq.a: abab6.5 is the larger, trillion-parameter model. abab6.5s is trained with the same techniques and data but optimized for efficiency and speed; MiniMax says it can process close to 30,000 words of text in about one second. Both share the same 200,000-token context window.

faq.q: How large is the context window?
faq.a: Both abab6.5 and abab6.5s support a 200,000-token context window, which was a long context for chat models at their April 2024 release.

faq.q: Are the abab6.5 models open weights?
faq.a: No. The abab6.5 series was a proprietary, API-only family served through MiniMax's platform, not released as downloadable weights. The line has since been superseded by MiniMax's MiniMax-01 and open-weight M-series models.
